title: Teyana Taylor on Oscars, Motherhood, and Never Caging a Creative
content:
Teyana Taylor drew heavily on her own postpartum experience to portray the complex character Perfidia in the 2023 movie A Thousand and One. Her performance highlights the painful reality of women not feeling heard, seen, or appreciated in their daily lives. In the film, Perfidia abandons her family to continue her activism, a controversial choice that has led some audiences to villainize her. However, Taylor believes that many women understand Perfidia's desperate need to finally put herself first. She notes that society constantly forces women to be supermoms and superwives without ever offering them a moment of grace.

Like Perfidia, Taylor knows exactly who she is and refuses to pick a single lane, even when industry insiders tell her to focus. When she briefly walked away from music in 2020 due to feeling underappreciated, many people called her decision foolish. However, she boldly declared her intentions to become a great actress and director, successfully proving her countless doubters wrong. Although she is now experiencing the immense pressure of Oscar nominations, Taylor insists that simply being recognized makes her a winner. She remains determined to have fun and bond with her peers in an industry that can often feel incredibly cold.

Taylor eventually returned to music because letting go of the pressure allowed her to genuinely fall back in love with the art. Navigating a public divorce and a new relationship gave her fresh, deeply personal material about heartbreak and healing. Despite her packed schedule of filming, touring, and culinary school, she remains incredibly ambitious about her future. She playfully dreams of achieving an EGOT, insisting that true creatives should never be caged or limited by expectations. By constantly spreading her wings, Taylor proves that a multifaceted artist can successfully conquer multiple industries at once.
